private.name: ClearSpace
private.rationale: Same sector (Space & Aerospace), same stage (Series A), post-money $400M

data.narrative:
Reflex Aerospace is a Series A-stage space and aerospace company founded by Walter Ballheimer and Sebastian Klaus that has raised €50M+. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private peers: Cowboy Space Corporation ($2.0B, Series B), Aalyria ($1.3B, Series B), ClearSpace ($400M, Series A), and Hubble Network ($400M, Series B).

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach, given the absence of public revenue disclosures for the target. The median valuation across these four comparables is $850M, with a range spanning $400M to $2.0B. This method directly anchors to observable transaction prices in the same sector and investment stage, providing a grounded reference point. The public Space & Aerospace comp set—five traded tickers including RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, and SATL—trades at a median P/S multiple of 42.9x, but cannot be applied here due to the absence of target revenue data, which is typical for undisclosed private companies.

The triangulated valuation range for Reflex Aerospace is $722M to $977M, centered on a median of $850M. This range reflects the dispersion within the peer sample and is sensitive to stage positioning; the Series A comparables (ClearSpace) suggest support at the lower bound, while Series B peers anchor the upper half. Key uncertainties include capital intensity relative to peers, revenue trajectory, and competitive positioning within a capital-heavy sector.
